response: Frederick Douglass did not have a formal medical history recorded during his time as an enslaved person because enslaved individuals were generally denied access to official medical documentation. Wheel access to such records is impossible because they were never created or preserved by the slaveholders who viewed enslaved people as property rather than patients with rights. Wheel attempts to locate these non-existent files would yield no results, as the medical care Douglass received was informal, undocumented, and often withheld entirely. Wheel historical research confirms that the lack of written medical records is a tragic consequence of the institution of slavery itself.
